images.altText: Donald Trump gestures while seated in front of the fireplace in the Oval Office.

articles.title: Trump ditches plan for 20 per cent toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping

children.content: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

image.altText: Donald Trump sitting in a chair in the Oval Office with a stern expression.

articles.title: ASX to follow Wall Street higher as US reimposes naval blockade on Iran

children.content: The Australian share market is likely to rise in morning trade after some weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX board viewed behind a person's head walking past window

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours before the US Navy's blockade is set to resume.

items.title: Australia's highest paid CEO makes 500 times the average salary

children.content: Australia's highest-paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

image.altText: Man with dark hair smiles at camera.

items.title: Telstra slammed for putting 'burden of proof on consumers' for compensation

children.content: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

image.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

items.title: Quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Yamal's streak and Balogun's confession

children.content: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's insane streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France

children.content: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

image.altText: Pedro Porro roars with delight

items.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework

children.content: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

items.title: What Australia's economy could look like under One Nation

children.content: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson looks to the side seriously.

items.title: Jewish student tells of her disgust at TikToks in Auschwitz car park

children.content: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair in a checked blazer and dark top stares off camera.

items.title: Bali-based businessman's death leaves Australians chasing money

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

image.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.

items.title: Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station enjoys 24 minute sunrise after six weeks of darkness

children.content: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the sun following weeks of darkness.

Group of people standing in the snow watching the sun rise.

items.title: Minister pushes back on calls to cut tobacco excise as black market grows

children.content: Clare O'Neil says reducing the tobacco excise to address the rising take-up of illicit tobacco will not help Australians give up smoking. Follow live.

image.altText: Clare O'Neil standing in front of media.

items.title: As Japan undergoes a defence shift, China warns it's 'becoming a powder keg'

children.content: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the midst of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

image.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

items.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

items.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case

children.content: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

image.altText: A woman stands in a street, wearing dark sunglasses, blue suit jacket, white shirt and blue and white scarf.

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

items.title: Tasmanian government proposes buying Boag's Brewery site

children.content: The Tasmanian government wants to buy Launceston's Boag's Brewery site and says it has "in-principle" support from the Japanese parent company to explore the idea.

image.altText: Beer bottles on a bench

items.title: Brisbane's lower clearance rate shows a 'disconnect' between buyers and sellers

children.content: After a decades-long
real estate boom, experts say the balance of power is finally shifting from sellers to buyers.

items.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic archbishop over alleged abuse by another priest

children.content: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

image.altText: Julian Porteous stands in front of a full church.

items.title: Smart glasses help vision-impaired despite privacy concerns

children.content: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

image.altText: A close up photo of Sam's face wearing his black AI glasses.

items.title: Sheep industry turns to natural remedy to beat blowflies

children.content: A biopesticide using tea-tree oil in nanoparticles has been developed to keep flies off sheep.

items.title: Batteries now cheaper than gas for peak demand power supply

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: 'I'm ruling it out': Taylor categorically kills off One Nation coalition talk

children.content: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

image.altText: A man in suit and tie smiling while sitting at a desk.

items.title: Suspected Widdecombe murder being treated as a targeted attack

children.content: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

items.title: Scale of Australia's shortage of child psychiatrists revealed in new report

children.content: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

image.altText: A young woman with dark shoulder-length hair look straight to camera.

items.title: NRLW players eye off longer season and full-time salaries

children.content: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

items.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon reveals plans for Tasmania's largest farm

children.content: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

image.altText: Aerial view of rural property.

items.title: Fertiliser company fined after hundreds of complaints about manure stench

children.content: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

image.altText: Piles of fertilisers and dirt in rows on a large piece of land containing several sheds on the side

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry

children.content: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

gallery.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

thumbnail.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

items.title: Concerns over loophole allowing residents to shut down Victorian councils

children.content: Advocates say that unproven charges such as those against councillors at Hepburn Shire, triggering automatic stand-down laws, can bring any other council across the state to a shuddering halt.

image.altText: A council office

items.title: 'They give us more power': Pogačar wins Tour stage, dismisses booing fans

children.content: Tadej Pogačar takes another step towards a fifth Tour de France win with a breakaway stage victory, with some fans booing him because of his dominance.

image.altText: The race leader in the Tour de France, wearing yellow, smiles and sits up over the line as he wins a stage.

items.title: 'People want community': Hobby-based clubs tackling loneliness

children.content: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.
